statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, write a function that reverses the order of its elements. The function should not use any built-in array methods (like `reverse()` or `splice()`).
statement.outputFormat: The input array with its elements in reverse order.
statement.notes: The function should not modify the original array.

constraints.description: The number of elements in the input array.

hints.text: Try to reverse the array by swapping elements from the start and end.

editorial.title: Editorial: Reversing an Array
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to use two pointers, one at the start and one at the end of the array.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to use a loop to swap each pair of elements.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use two pointers, one at the start and one at the end of the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use two pointers, one at the start and one at the end of the array, and swap the elements at these positions.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by induction.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Swap the first and last elements, then swap the second and second last elements, and so on.
